title: Intense Malty Polish Beef Stew
description: A hearty Polish stew featuring feather blade beef and a rich malty flavor, made with a mantecare-emulsify technique and ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly ingredients. This dish is perfect for a cold winter evening.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the beef cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the browned beef from the pot and set it aside.
Add the chopped onions to the pot and cook until they are softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes to the pot, cooking for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the malt syrup, allspice, and bay leaves to the pot, stirring to combine.
Add the beef broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
Return the browned beef to the pot and reduce the heat to low, simmering, covered, for 2.5 hours.
Stir in the sour cream and cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the stew has thickened slightly.
